jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer's workplace mishap
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation followed by screaming
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase delivery with perfect economy and character integration

jokes.text: Need more snooze. [Muttering]
jokes.setup: Alarm clock going off, Homer hits snooze
jokes.punchline: Homer's laziness expressed through childish language
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment that establishes Homer's laziness efficiently

jokes.text: Oh, I'm 26 hours late for work. No time for Maggie.
jokes.setup: Homer realizing he's late for work
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity of '26 hours' - more than a full day
jokes.explanation: Absurd time measurement combined with Homer's skewed priorities creates layered humor

jokes.text: This would be a lot easier without all these people.
jokes.setup: Homer looking for Waldo in a Where's Waldo book
jokes.punchline: Missing the entire point of the puzzle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the Where's Waldo concept showing Homer's fundamental misunderstanding

jokes.text: How'd you like a Details magazine up the wazoo?
jokes.setup: Road rage incident
jokes.punchline: Threatening someone with a lifestyle magazine instead of violence
jokes.explanation: Unexpected escalation using a specific pop culture reference as a weapon

jokes.text: Oh, sure. Single me out.
jokes.setup: Police siren wailing, presumably for Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer acting persecuted when he's clearly in the wrong
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer victimization complex when caught doing something wrong

jokes.text: Okay. You just bought yourself a 3-17: pointing out police stupidity. Or is that a 3-14? Nah, nah. 3-14 is a dog, uh- No. Or is that a 3-15? You're in trouble, pal!
jokes.setup: Police officer trying to cite Homer for pointing out their mistake
jokes.punchline: Officer's complete confusion about their own citation codes
jokes.explanation: Escalating confusion that reveals the officer's incompetence while trying to assert authority

jokes.text: Big ugly nose. Bald. Liver spot. Liver spot.
jokes.setup: Homer describing someone while looking in a mirror
jokes.punchline: He's describing himself without realizing it
jokes.explanation: Classic visual irony with Homer's complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: No! I can't get in trouble if I can't see you!
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns catching Homer late
jokes.punchline: Homer's childish logic about avoiding consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect capture of Homer's childlike reasoning and magical thinking

jokes.text: I knew I had those installed for a reason.
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns putting on leg protectors after kicking Homer
jokes.punchline: Burns casually revealing he planned for violent encounters
jokes.explanation: Dark humor revealing Burns' calculated cruelty and preparedness for violence

jokes.text: Yeah. And even a teaspoon could cause a fatal tumor.
jokes.setup: Discussing the toxic waste Homer has to eat
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual acknowledgment of the deadly danger
jokes.explanation: Dark workplace humor with Homer's matter-of-fact delivery of horrifying information

jokes.text: The cooling tank just blew, and they're taking Lenny to the hospital. Oh, no! Not Lenny! Not Lenny!
jokes.setup: Homer lying about a workplace emergency
jokes.punchline: Cut to him laughing with Lenny at the bowling alley
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff revealing Homer's deception with the visual contradiction

jokes.text: Are 'Poo' and 'Ass' taken? Yeah. Damn! Could my life get any worse?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to choose a silly bowling name
jokes.punchline: His disappointment at not getting juvenile humor names
jokes.explanation: Homer's juvenile sense of humor and dramatic overreaction to minor disappointment

jokes.text: Even for a bowler, you're fat.
jokes.setup: Insult directed at Homer
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging bowlers are generally fat, but Homer exceeds that
jokes.explanation: Efficient insult that plays on bowling stereotypes while targeting Homer specifically

jokes.text: Ah, actually, I'd say you're having a severe psychotic episode.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about seeing Burns's face
jokes.punchline: Carl's matter-of-fact diagnosis of psychosis
jokes.explanation: Carl's deadpan delivery of serious psychological assessment in casual context

jokes.text: Hey, if that's psychotic, then why am I takin' these?
jokes.setup: Carl diagnosing Homer with psychosis
jokes.punchline: Homer implying he's on medication, which proves the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect logical loop where Homer's defense actually confirms the diagnosis

jokes.text: Will you at least call it a 'banana split,' you dumb wad?
jokes.setup: Lenny complaining about a split (bowling term) he bought
jokes.punchline: Carl's frustration at the terminology confusion
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on bowling/food terminology but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Hey, spare me your gutter mouth!
jokes.setup: Continuing the bowling alley conversation
jokes.punchline: Using bowling terms (spare, gutter) in regular insults
jokes.explanation: Simple bowling pun that's functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: We knew it contained spider eggs... but the hantavirus- well, that really came out of left field.
jokes.setup: Krusty discussing his dangerous gum product
jokes.punchline: Treating deadly virus as an unexpected bonus problem
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from known terrible to surprisingly worse, with perfect Krusty character voice

jokes.text: This could be the greatest individual achievement in the history of Springfield. Which proves just how pitiful this town is.
jokes.setup: Praising Homer's potential perfect game
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercutting it by insulting Springfield
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and immediate deflation, showing Kent's cynical character while commenting on Springfield

jokes.text: Why won't they come over? I'm so lonely.
jokes.setup: Marge telling family to stay away so Homer can concentrate
jokes.punchline: Homer wants the opposite - attention during his big moment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's need for attention conflicting with performance pressure

jokes.text: This is as fast as I can move.
jokes.setup: Someone asking what's wrong with slow-moving Grandpa
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact explanation of his physical limitations
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective age humor with Grandpa's deadpan honesty about his limitations

jokes.text: In your face, to-do list!
jokes.setup: Homer celebrating his perfect game
jokes.punchline: Addressing an inanimate object as if it were a person he defeated
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - treating completing a task as defeating an enemy

jokes.text: But there was someone else with me on that alley. I'm talkin' about the big man- Carl!
jokes.setup: Homer building up to thank someone important
jokes.punchline: It's just Carl, who he calls 'the big man'
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection deflating the expected heartfelt moment

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson bowled a perfect game... without the aid of steroids, crack, angel dust... or the other narcotics that are synonymous with pro bowling.
jokes.setup: Teacher praising Homer's achievement
jokes.punchline: Casually listing serious drugs as normal bowling aids
jokes.explanation: Unexpected dark humor about professional bowling's alleged drug culture

jokes.text: Will you be my dad? [Chuckling] You've got a father. He's just a dud.
jokes.setup: Child asking Homer to be his father
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt assessment of the kid's actual father
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual cruelty delivered with complete matter-of-fact tone

jokes.text: Pumpkin face? Headgear? Chicken pox? Smelly? Lazy eye? Spikyhead?
jokes.setup: Homer calling on students
jokes.punchline: Using cruel nicknames instead of actual names
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual cruelty to children escalating through increasingly mean nicknames

jokes.text: There's a stairway, dumb-ass!
jokes.setup: Homer apparently climbing to his square the wrong way
jokes.punchline: Ron Howard's exasperated correction
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with Homer missing obvious solutions

jokes.text: Well, that- Uh, well, wait. Uh, do I read from the sheet labeled 'jokes' or 'answers'?
jokes.setup: Homer asked a game show question
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall by asking about his script sheets
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor that breaks the fourth wall while staying in character

jokes.text: Flavor of the month? Me? Yeah, Homer. You can't just ride one accomplishment forever.
jokes.setup: Homer questioning his celebrity status
jokes.punchline: Ron Howard's blunt assessment of Homer's fleeting fame
jokes.explanation: Good reality check delivered with Hollywood insider knowledge

jokes.text: Why do you think I stopped acting and became a director? I don't know. 'Cause you weren't cute anymore?
jokes.setup: Ron Howard making a point about career evolution
jokes.punchline: Homer's brutally honest assessment of Howard's looks
jokes.explanation: Excellent celebrity roast humor with Homer's innocent cruelty hitting perfectly

jokes.text: Hello, everybody! Did somebody say 'A perfect game'?
jokes.setup: Penn and Teller performing a dangerous magic trick
jokes.punchline: Homer interrupting with his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Homer's complete lack of situational awareness

jokes.text: Hey, I thought you never talked. Uh, I didn't mean to. Huh. It just slipped out.
jokes.setup: Homer addressing Teller, who's famously silent
jokes.punchline: Teller accidentally breaking character and speaking
jokes.explanation: Clever joke about Teller's real-life silent persona being broken by Homer's disruption

jokes.text: He'll do it. I'm not the first Teller.
jokes.setup: Penn threatening to kill someone
jokes.punchline: Implication that Penn has killed previous Tellers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor suggesting Penn and Teller's act involves actual murder

jokes.text: Are you as bored as I am with the antics of this warmed-over Fred Flintstone? I say it's time to put this one-trick pony out to stud.
jokes.setup: Kent introducing a segment about Homer
jokes.punchline: Brutal comparison to Fred Flintstone and mixed metaphor about horses
jokes.explanation: Good media satire with Kent's cynical dismissal and confused metaphor

jokes.text: They did it to Jesus, and now they're doing it to me. Are you comparing yourself to our Lord? Well in bowling ability
jokes.setup: Homer comparing his situation to Jesus
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he only means in bowling ability
jokes.explanation: Brilliant blasphemous humor with perfect setup and absurd clarification that somehow makes it worse

jokes.text: As your life partner, I'm very worried.
jokes.setup: Moe responding to Homer's depression
jokes.punchline: Calling himself Homer's 'life partner'
jokes.explanation: Good character humor about the closeness of Homer and Moe's relationship

jokes.text: A shred of what? Sorry. I was counting the cocktail radishes. Now, where was I? Uh, two, three- three radishes. Three big radishes.
jokes.setup: Homer saying something about hope
jokes.punchline: Moe completely missing emotional moment while obsessing over radishes
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Moe's bartender obsessiveness over tiny details

jokes.text: Yeah, less chat, more splat, pal!
jokes.setup: Homer about to jump off bridge
jokes.punchline: Bystander encouraging suicide with rhyming phrase
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with efficient rhyming phrase that's shockingly callous

jokes.text: Ooh! Morlocks. C.H.U.D.'s. Mole people! There is no escape from the fortress of the moles!
jokes.setup: Homer falling into underground tunnels
jokes.punchline: Cataloging various underground monster movie references
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference humor with escalating monster movie tropes

jokes.text: Not anymore, Ron. Now I'm looking for something to dedicate my life to. Something noble, but easy.
jokes.setup: Ron Howard asking if Homer is trying to kill himself
jokes.punchline: Homer's contradictory criteria for his new life purpose
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Homer's desire for meaning with minimal effort

jokes.text: Well, it's a different zoo containing animals you've never heard of.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting that even big stars take kids to the zoo
jokes.punchline: Ron Howard's pretentious correction about exclusive exotic animals
jokes.explanation: Good satire of Hollywood elite privilege with absurd exclusivity

jokes.text: Daddy, we're missing the Fantastapotamus. She only sings twice a day.
jokes.setup: Ron Howard talking about his exclusive zoo
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up fantastical animal with specific performance schedule
jokes.explanation: Excellent invented creature name that escalates the absurdity of celebrity privilege

jokes.text: Usually when you say that, you give the money back. I do what now?
jokes.setup: Homer saying he doesn't want pity money
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion about the normal social convention
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's selective understanding of social norms

jokes.text: Over the years, I've learned to find father figures wherever I can. Construction workers, the Internet and Nelson here.
jokes.setup: Homer offering to spend more time with Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's disturbing list of substitute father figures
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about Bart's neglect with escalating inappropriateness of father figures

jokes.text: Hi, Maggie. I'm Homie-Womie, the Teletubbie. And I'm all man, in case you heard otherwise.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to bond with Maggie through role-play
jokes.punchline: Defensive clarification about his masculinity while dressed as a Teletubby
jokes.explanation: Good character humor about Homer's fragile masculinity even while playing with his baby

jokes.text: See, Maggie? The ocean is just like a bathtub. Except instead of rubber duckies... it has barracudas and moray eels.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to comfort Maggie about the ocean
jokes.punchline: Comparing harmless bath toys to dangerous sea creatures
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke that shows Homer's terrible parenting through misplaced reassurance

jokes.text: I know. If I sink to the bottom, I can run to the shore.
jokes.setup: Homer struggling against the riptide
jokes.punchline: Completely illogical solution based on cartoon physics
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic that ignores basic physics in favor of cartoon thinking

jokes.text: Maggie, call Aquaman!
jokes.setup: Homer drowning and desperate
jokes.punchline: Asking a baby to call a fictional superhero for help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer desperation logic combining impossible requests in one efficient line

jokes.text: Actually, that was before I went in the ocean. I don't want to pry into your personal life- Then don't!
jokes.setup: Doctor mentioning Homer swallowed shark eggs
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive response to reasonable concern
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's defensive reaction to judgment about his eating habits

jokes.text: But you stepped over the line just a little bit... so I'll have to give you a five. And that gives you a final score of... 295.
jokes.setup: Homer praising baby Maggie's bowling
jokes.punchline: Somehow scoring her terrible performance as near-perfect
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Homer's love for Maggie through impossible scoring generosity
